evals.name: vague-cleanup-redirect
evals.prompt: honestly this checkout component is a bit of a mess, can you clean it up and make it more maintainable?
evals.expected_output: One short line naming the concrete target in checkout.jsx and a concrete change (e.g. useReducer), phrased so 'yes' is enough. No verbose refactor essay.

evals.name: multi-concept-menu
evals.prompt: can you refactor auth.js — fix up the token refresh, add rate limiting on login, move the session store to redis, and improve the error messages?
evals.expected_output: A terse numbered menu of the 4 changes, asking which to start, parking the rest. No planning essay, no implementing all four.

evals.name: concrete-debounce
evals.prompt: add a 300ms debounce to the search input handler in SearchBar.jsx so we're not firing a request on every keystroke
evals.expected_output: Code-first: a diff/snippet adding the debounce plus import, with little or no prose. No preamble.

evals.name: ripcord-explain
evals.prompt: why would you use useReducer instead of all these separate useState calls here? explain the tradeoffs
evals.expected_output: A full, clear explanation of the tradeoffs (the skill steps aside when 'why/explain' is asked). NOT a terse refusal.

evals.name: architecture-recommendation
evals.prompt: we're hitting scaling limits on the orders service — single Postgres, around 5k writes/sec at peak and climbing. should we shard the db, add read replicas, or move to event sourcing? what would you do?
evals.expected_output: Engages with a real recommendation (architecture discussion is allowed). Lays out the options concisely and picks one with a short reason. NOT a terse refusal, but NOT a 700-word essay either — concrete levers, clear pick.

evals.name: vague-complex-sloppy
evals.prompt: this dashboard.js file is a complete disaster honestly, can you sort it out?
evals.expected_output: Does NOT dump a full rewrite. Names the worst concrete problems as a short list/menu (callback nesting, var, no error handling, XSS via innerHTML, mixed fetch+DOM, global state) and asks which to tackle first, or proposes one concrete first slice. Concise.

evals.name: architecture-vibe-redirect
evals.prompt: i feel like our whole data layer is poorly architected and it's slowing us down. thoughts?
evals.expected_output: Borderline: 'thoughts?' invites discussion, but the ask is vague. Ideal: engages briefly but pulls toward something concrete — asks which part (ORM, query patterns, caching, N+1) or to point at a file — rather than a long abstract essay.
